description: Pharmacology interpretations - drug metabolism, pharmacogenetics, medication sensitivities

AC.interpretation: Moderate caffeine sensitivity - longer lasting effects

CC.interpretation: Prolonged caffeine effects - reduced drug metabolism rate
CC.theWhy: Your CYP1A2 variant slows drug metabolism, requiring careful dosing and timing
CC.scienceSimplified: Your liver processes medications more slowly, so drugs stay active longer in your system

CC.interpretation: Standard CYP2C19 promoter — normal enzyme expression

CT.interpretation: Increased CYP2C19 expression — faster drug clearance

TT.interpretation: Significantly increased CYP2C19 — very rapid drug clearance

GG.interpretation: Standard CYP2C19 activity — normal clopidogrel activation

GA.interpretation: Reduced CYP2C19 activity — lower clopidogrel active metabolite
GA.action: CPIC Level A: Consider prasugrel or ticagrelor instead of clopidogrel

AA.action: CPIC Level A: Clopidogrel ineffective. Use prasugrel or ticagrelor

recommendations: Standard caution with narrow therapeutic drugs
May have reduced response to codeine/oxycodone
May have reduced response to some drugs
Dose adjustments needed for many medications

TC.interpretation: *41 - splice site variant with reduced function

CC.interpretation: Standard CYP2C9 activity — normal warfarin metabolism
recommendations: Standard warfarin dosing based on clinical factors

CT.interpretation: Reduced warfarin clearance ~30% — increased bleeding risk
CT.action: CPIC Level A: Reduce warfarin dose 20-30%. Monitor INR frequently

TT.interpretation: Severely reduced warfarin clearance — high bleeding risk
TT.action: CPIC Level A: Reduce warfarin dose 40-50%. Strongly consider DOAC alternative

AC.action: CPIC Level A: Reduce warfarin dose. Monitor INR

CC.interpretation: Very low CYP2C9 activity — severe warfarin sensitivity
CC.action: CPIC Level A: Significant warfarin dose reduction or alternative anticoagulant required

recommendations: May have faster clearance of CYP3A4 substrates
May need higher doses for some drugs
May need lower doses of some drugs

AA.interpretation: Can express CYP3A5 enzyme - normal metabolism of tacrolimus, sirolimus
recommendations: Standard tacrolimus dosing may be effective
May need dose adjustments for calcineurin inhibitors

GG.interpretation: Cannot express functional CYP3A5 - relies on CYP3A4 only

TT.interpretation: Rapid inactivation of isoniazid, sulfonamides, hydralazine

recommendations: May need more frequent dosing for some drugs
Increased risk of isoniazid peripheral neuropathy

AA.interpretation: Homozygous poor metabolizer - high risk of myelosuppression

recommendations: Avoid thiopurines or use very low doses

GA.interpretation: Reduced fluorouracil clearance — increased toxicity risk
GA.action: CPIC Level A: Reduce fluoropyrimidine dose 50%

AA.interpretation: Severe DPD deficiency — life-threatening toxicity risk with standard doses
AA.action: CPIC Level A: CONTRAINDICATED — avoid fluorouracil and capecitabine

recommendations: Fluoropyrimidine toxicity risk - avoid standard dosing

AA.interpretation: No *28 - normal bilirubin metabolism, irinotecan tolerance

GG.interpretation: Homozygous *28 - Gilbert syndrome - reduced irinotecan clearance
recommendations: Reduced irinotecan dose may be needed
Consider dose reduction for affected drugs

GG.interpretation: Homozygous *37 - Crigler-Najjar type II or Gilbert syndrome
recommendations: Increased risk of hyperbilirubinemia with certain drugs

TT.interpretation: Normal hepatic statin uptake — standard myopathy risk

TC.interpretation: Reduced hepatic statin uptake — increased myopathy risk
TC.action: CPIC Level A: Consider lower simvastatin dose or alternative statin

CC.interpretation: Severely reduced hepatic statin uptake — 17x increased myopathy risk with high-dose simvastatin
CC.action: CPIC Level A: High myopathy risk with simvastatin. Use alternative statin

TT.interpretation: Significantly reduced hepatic uptake - high statin levels

GG.interpretation: Standard VKORC1 expression — standard warfarin dose requirement

GA.interpretation: Reduced VKORC1 expression — requires ~30% lower warfarin dose

AA.interpretation: Very low VKORC1 expression — requires ~50% lower warfarin dose

CG.interpretation: Reduced NUDT15 activity - increased thiopurine toxicity risk

GG.interpretation: Homozygous variant - high risk of severe myelosuppression
recommendations: Avoid thiopurines or use very low doses

del/del.interpretation: No functional enzyme - extreme thiopurine sensitivity

CT.interpretation: Moderately reduced P-gp - affects digoxin, many other drugs

recommendations: Lower digoxin doses may be needed
May have altered response to P-gp substrates
Consult physician for drug dosing adjustments

GA.interpretation: Reduced CYP2B6 activity - slower drug clearance

recommendations: Increased CNS toxicity risk at standard doses
Smokers may need higher drug doses

GG.interpretation: CC genotype - better response to pegylated interferon for HCV
recommendations: Standard hepatitis C treatment likely effective

TT.interpretation: TT genotype - poorer response to interferon-based HCV treatment
recommendations: Consider newer DAAs instead of interferon-based regimens
Monitor drug response for P-gp substrates
Some drugs may have altered bioavailability

*.interpretation: Relevant to Tramadol response. Pharmacogenomic variant affecting drug metabolism or response. Clinical significance: drug response. Review status: no assertion criteria provided.
recommendations: Discuss pharmacogenomic findings with prescribing physician
Tramadol metabolism may be altered — consider dose adjustment or alternative analgesic
Include pharmacogenomic profile in medical record for future prescribing decisions

TT.effect: High FKBP5 expression — slow glucocorticoid receptor reset
TT.interpretation: Slower HPA axis recovery after stress — elevated PTSD risk, reduced antidepressant response, prolonged cortisol exposure after stressors
recommendations: Phosphatidylserine 400mg (blunts cortisol response to exercise stress)
Strict sleep schedule — stabilises diurnal cortisol rhythm
HRV biofeedback for autonomic resilience training

TC.interpretation: Moderate HPA axis recovery delay — partial stress response sensitization

CC.effect: Normal FKBP5 expression — efficient GR reset
CC.interpretation: Normal stress recovery — glucocorticoid receptor resensitizes efficiently after cortisol exposure

CC.effect: High CYP1A1 inducibility — more carcinogen activation
CC.interpretation: Higher induction of CYP1A1 by PAHs — disproportionate conversion of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (tobacco smoke, charred food) into DNA-reactive epoxide carcinogens
recommendations: Avoid tobacco smoke entirely — risk disproportionately elevated vs. other genotypes
Minimize charred, smoked, and barbecued foods
Cruciferous vegetables and sulforaphane inhibit CYP1A1 induction

TC.interpretation: One high-inducibility allele — moderately elevated carcinogen activation risk
recommendations: Limit smoke and charred food exposure

TT.interpretation: Standard carcinogen activation — normal PAH metabolism

AA.effect: Low VKORC1 expression — very high warfarin sensitivity
AA.interpretation: High warfarin sensitivity — requires significantly lower maintenance dose (~2-3mg/day vs 5mg standard); bleeding risk at standard doses
recommendations: CRITICAL: Inform all prescribing physicians before warfarin or acenocoumarol therapy
More frequent INR monitoring when initiating anticoagulation
Carry genetic result card to all healthcare encounters

GA.effect: Intermediate VKORC1 expression — moderate warfarin sensitivity
GA.interpretation: Moderately increased warfarin sensitivity — typically requires ~30% lower dose than population average
recommendations: Inform prescribers of this variant for anticoagulant therapy

GG.effect: Normal VKORC1 expression — standard warfarin dose
GG.interpretation: Standard warfarin sensitivity — typical maintenance dose (~5mg/day) expected
High PON1 activity — efficient hydrolysis of oxidized LDL phospholipids, organophosphate pesticides, and homocysteine thiolactone
recommendations: Maintain HDL levels (PON1 travels on HDL); regular aerobic exercise
Extra-virgin olive oil polyphenols upregulate PON1 expression
Pomegranate juice — shown to increase PON1 activity

AG.interpretation: Intermediate paraoxonase activity — moderate protection against lipid oxidation
recommendations: Olive oil and pomegranate support PON1 activity

AA.interpretation: Reduced PON1 enzyme activity — lower protection against oxidized LDL and organophosphate toxins; increased cardiovascular and pesticide sensitivity risk
recommendations: Extra-virgin olive oil and pomegranate daily to maximize PON1 expression
Regular HDL monitoring; target HDL > 60 mg/dL
